description: In January 2019, Gillette released a Super Bowl-adjacent commercial titled 'We Believe: The Best Men Can Be,' which addressed toxic masculinity and #MeToo. The ad triggered a massive polarization of the brand's customer base, leading to widespread boycott calls and intense social media discourse regarding the company's shift in corporate messaging.

key_events.description: Gillette releases the ad on YouTube and social channels, triggering immediate backlash on Twitter and Reddit.
Mainstream media outlets begin coverage, framing the debate as a culture war, which fuels the negative sentiment among core demographics.
P&G leadership defends the campaign, citing long-term brand equity goals over short-term sales dips.
Competitors observe slight market share fluctuations, leading to a stabilization of the brand's position as the controversy wanes.

calibration_notes: This scenario is ideal for calibrating models on 'values-based marketing' and the risk-reward ratio of corporate activism in highly polarized political climates.
